name: Woodrow Wilson
description: president of the United States from 1913 to 1921 (1856–1924)
abstract: Thomas Woodrow Wilson (December 28, 1856 – February 3, 1924) was the 28th president of the United States, serving from 1913 to 1921. He was the only Democrat to serve as president during the Progressive Era, when Republicans dominated the presidency and legislative branches.

concerts.label: Meeting: Protest Against Russian Passport Discrimination

about_quotes.speaker: Amos White
about_quotes.evidence: In 1912, for Woodrow Wilson, the Democrats came to power and the Democrats in South Carolina didn't want the Jenkins-Ownage band to appear in this inauguration parade.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: We had it again in the first World War, when Woodrow Wilson practically suspended the First Amendment.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
